name: memoryai-vsx
description: One brain. Every AI you use. Forever. Shared long-term memory for Claude, Cursor, AI assistant, and any IDE that follows.

memoryai.endpoint.description: MemoryAI API endpoint. Use http://localhost:8420 for self-hosted.

memoryai.compactMode.description: How the context-guard picks its compact/critical triggers. auto = detect the model's window from 'Model' below and pick the trigger automatically (≤200K window → 95%, >200K → 30%). manual = set a single 'Compact At Tokens' below (the soft point where the guard starts saving); the hard critical ceiling is derived automatically at 1.2× of that. Note: VS Code's Settings page always shows every field; the unrelated fields are simply ignored in the mode you are not using. The MemoryAI Connect panel hides them for you.
enumDescriptions: Auto-detect from the model name and pick the trigger automatically.
Use the exact compact token count you set below (critical derived at 1.2×).

memoryai.model.description: Auto mode only (ignored when Compact Mode = manual). Your model name so MemoryAI auto-detects its context window and picks the right compact trigger (≤200K window → 95%, >200K → 30%). Examples: claude-sonnet-4-6, claude-opus-4-8[1m], gemini-1.5-pro. Leave blank to use the 200K default.

memoryai.compactAtTokens.description: Manual mode only (ignored when Compact Mode = auto). The soft point in tokens at which the guard starts saving (compact). The hard critical ceiling that forces a compact fires automatically at 1.2× of this. Example: on a 1M-window model set 160000 (→ critical at 192000) or 500000 (→ critical at 600000). 0 = let the server decide.

memoryai.privateMode.description: Local-only mode for NDA / regulated data. Reserved for a future release.

memoryai.statusBar.description: Status-bar display format. full = brain size + monthly activity + savings (recommended). savings = $ saved today only. tokens = brain volume only. compact = monthly activity only. off = hide.
enumDescriptions: Brain volume — total memories and storage.
This month's activity — stores and recalls.
